intro: Helicteres dumosa, also known as the "caatinga tree," is a flowering plant species native to the Caatinga dry forests of northeastern Brazil. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties.
